_schema.description: Sankey diagram data: nodes represent stages or entities in a flow system; links represent directed flows between them. Values are in exajoules (EJ). Replace with real flow data in the same unit.

fields.nodes[].id: string — unique key, must match link source/target
fields.nodes[].label: string — display name on the diagram
fields.nodes[].col: number — column index (0 = leftmost stage, 1 = middle, 2 = rightmost). Controls horizontal position.
fields.links[].source: string — node id of the flow origin
fields.links[].target: string — node id of the flow destination
fields.links[].value: number — flow quantity (must be positive; unit matches dataset)

_schema.replace: Swap node ids/labels and link values with your real flow data. Add or remove columns by changing the col values. The layout algorithm re-derives all positions from scratch on each render — no coordinates needed. Losses nodes (col 2 with no outgoing links) should be named 'losses' or similar to receive the bottom-of-column placement heuristic.
